context_summary: Plan 12-07 SHIPPED (commit e4ab92f, 22 commits): production-ready /get on HTTP+TCP via mio apply_shard + main.rs migration to ServerV18 + dispatch_get_batch real impl + OP_GET_RESPONSE=0x0023 + /health shim on mio listener. Phase 19.4 read-bench unblocked. Next session: review/execute one of three follow-up plans (12-08 apply-overhead reduction, 12-09 TCP /get msgpack, 12-10 push-and-get on mio).

completed_this_session.result: /get production-ready HTTP+TCP via mio; main.rs migrated to ServerV18; dispatch_get_batch real impl; OP_GET_RESPONSE=0x0023; /health shim; 22 TDD-paired commits
Apple-M4 fraud-team P=4/PD=64: 86k EPS push + 6.13ms /get p99; saturated 89k+229ms; small/sat 696k+44ms
Read throughput sweep: fraud-team 9,575 r/s peak (32 workers); small 61,888 r/s peak; ~209 ns/cell fraud / ~32 ns/cell small

completed_this_session.item: bench-v18 single-key fetch sweep + cost model
completed_this_session.result: Single-cell 1×1: 175,843 r/s peak Apple-M4 / 196,548 r/s Hetzner. Cost model: ~5.5 µs fixed per req + ~210 ns/cell
Apply-thread per-/get work: p50=542ns mean=1.21µs. parse 23%, resolve 15%, lock 8%, lookup 23%, serialize 31%
Hetzner 1-client (P=16) = 51,131 EPS; 8-client (P=2 each) = 51,381 EPS aggregate. Confirms server is the bottleneck not bench-side mmap_lock

Apply at 3.6% CPU push / 11% read. Real work is 47% (push) / 9% (read) of apply CPU; rest is orchestration overhead (channel/mio/kernel-wakeup/allocator). Bench-side allocator (osq_lock+mprotect+malloc+BytesMut) hits 19% on Hetzner saturated push
12-08 (apply-overhead) 1629 lines / 12 tasks / 6 waves; 12-09 (TCP msgpack /get) 932 lines / 8 waves; 12-10 (push-and-get mio) 2158 lines / 23 tasks / 11 waves. All red-green TDD-paired.

decisions_locked_for_12_08.decision: Trace gates PASS, EPS gates STRETCH
decisions_locked_for_12_08.rationale: EPS lift gated by bench-side+kernel-TCP capacity; trace deltas (try_to_wake_up<1%, run_mio_event_loop<5%, malloc<0.5%) we directly control
Process-level rusage flakes from other-thread noise

decisions_locked_for_12_08.decision: RecyclableBytes wrapper (Drop returns BytesMut to pool)
decisions_locked_for_12_08.rationale: User chose performant variant; zero malloc per response on hot path; falls back to simple buffer-pool design as escape hatch
CLAUDE.md mandates criterion microbench; samply traces are complementary
Comprehensive baselines for Phase 13 ship gate

decisions_locked_for_12_10.rationale: Slot after OP_GET_RESPONSE=0x0023; both sync and async modes use it; difference is fsync timing

context_notes: Big session. Plan 12-07 shipped end-to-end with 22 commits + benchmarks + 13 follow-up commits. Three downstream plans scoped + written. The headline finding from perf traces: at saturated push the apply thread is only 3.6% CPU on Hetzner (server is fundamentally underutilized; bench-side allocator is the visible bottleneck via osq_lock+mprotect contention). Plan 12-08 expects to drive apply to ~100% via busy-poll + drain + batching + RecyclableBytes pool, opening ~1.7× theoretical apply ceiling. Plan 12-09 reduces dispatch_get_batch p50 by ~50% via msgpack. Plan 12-10 collapses fraud-decisioning hot path from 2 RTs to 1. Combined estimated: push 51k→95k EPS Hetzner (1.85×), single-cell read 196k→360k r/s Hetzner (1.83×); fraud-decisioning P50 latency 500µs→250µs. ALL within locked architectural constraints (single apply thread, mio data plane, no same-key sketch batching, read-after-write atomicity).
